generation.method: exact react npm tarball plus local Fast React package copied into a temporary node_modules tree
generation.probeIsolation: one Node child process per target, scenario, and mode

probeModes.reason: Default Node development element objects expose warning getters, debug fields, and frozen objects.
Default Node production element objects are mutable and omit development diagnostics.
The react-server condition has separate React and JSX runtime entrypoint behavior in development.
The react-server condition has separate React and JSX runtime entrypoint behavior in production.

captures: multiple children array identity and freeze state
own undefined key shadowing inherited key

@fast-react/react.source: local workspace package copied for behavior comparison

args.value: %s: `key` is not a prop. Trying to access it will result in `undefined` being returned. If you need to access the same value within the child component, you should pass it as a different prop. (https://react.dev/link/special-props)
Accessing element.ref was removed in React 19. ref is now a regular prop. It will be removed from the JSX Element type in a future release.

error.name: TypeError
error.message: Cannot convert a Symbol value to a string

args.value: The provided key is an unsupported type %s. This value must be coerced to a string before using it here.

error.name: Error
error.message: The argument must be a React element, but you passed null.

error.name: Error
error.message: The argument must be a React element, but you passed undefined.
